New file src/lib/utils/recentCharactersBackends.ts defines RecentCharactersBackend interface (name + load() + add()) with a discriminated AddOutcome status ('ok' | 'unauthorized' | 'unavailable'). execution_log.note: Added a centralized handleApiError(scope, error, clientMessage?, status?) helper to src/lib/server/logger.ts. It logs the full error server-side via logServerError (which forwards the original error object so stack traces survive) and returns a sanitized {error: string} response via apiError. Migrated all 6 generic top-level catch blocks in routes/api to use it: death-hotspots, damage-average, spec-statistics, spec-top-players, top-players, browse-logs. WCL handlers keep their two-branch pattern (WclQueryError vs unexpected) by design — graphql/http distinctions need the kind discriminator. JSDoc on handleApiError documents the convention. npm run check passes.

execution_log.note: Documented the codebase-wide error handling convention as a new section 7 in DESIGN.md ('Error Handling Conventions'). The doc nails down the policy per layer: (1) API handlers must use apiError/apiOk + handleApiError(scope, error, clientMessage?) — never bare json({error}) or unlogged catches; (2) DB layer must wrap every query in dbOperation(label, fn), return empty domain values only for 'no rows, success', never to mask failures; (3) logging goes through logServerError/logServerWarn with '<layer>/<name>' scope tags; (4) client-side utils return discriminated unions (kind:'ok'|'empty'|'error') instead of throwing or returning null-as-error. The 'Throw vs return: rule of thumb' subsection explicitly resolves the original finding's complaint: throw for contract violations (DB/WCL/env), return discriminated union for domain empty-vs-error states, return empty value only inside dbOperation for documented no-rows semantics. The conventions match the actual code after this session's refactors (apiError/apiOk, handleApiError, dbOperation, requireSession, executeWclQuery, fetchRuns/fetchWowSummary discriminated unions).

execution_log.note: Bulk error-handling unification + recents store refactor + score-source registry; resolves 11 review findings.

Documented the three patterns in DESIGN.md §8 'API Endpoint Naming': (1) Resource (default, kebab-case noun or modifier-noun like recent-characters, top-players, character-score, boss-events); (2) Provider passthrough (thin proxy where route name IS the upstream provider, e.g. blizzard, raiderio); (3) RPC (verb-noun, used sparingly for action-shaped endpoints that compute/trigger rather than fetch a resource — calculate-runs, browse-logs). Documented kebab-case requirement, no trailing slash / no version prefix, and stability rule for public endpoints (calculate-runs is externally documented on the rating-calculator page). Renaming the existing endpoints is a breaking change for /api/calculate-runs which is a documented public integration point, so the resolution is to codify the convention so future routes don't drift, rather than rename. Section cross-references §7 for response shape.

Both connections are intentionally separate and now share their config through src/lib/db/connection.ts. The 'similar config' the reviewer flagged was actual duplication before this session — that is now fixed: createDrizzlePostgres({label, connectionString, connectionStringSource}) owns the per-request, no-pooling Postgres+Drizzle factory (prepare:false, max:1, idle_timeout:5, connect_timeout:10, max_lifetime:30, etc.). getRaidDb() (src/lib/db/index.ts, 7 lines) and getUserDb() (src/lib/db/userDb.ts, 7 lines) are now thin wrappers that supply their connection string and a label that flows into logger scopes ('db/raid' vs 'db/user'). The separation is intentional — they connect to two different deployed Postgres instances (DATABASE_URL = read-only raid analytics; DATABASE_USER_URL = user/auth). Documented at: connection.ts module doc (explains the aggressive no-pool config and why two connections exist), JSDoc headers on schema.ts/userSchema.ts cross-referencing each other, drizzle/README.md mapping each schema to its config + migration directory, and DESIGN.md §9 'Library Layout'. The factory pattern means future per-DB tweaks happen in exactly one place. Commit cdd7a11.

execution_log.note: Extract shared token cache factory.

Made the shared createTokenCache factory concurrency-safe and explicitly documented the singleton rationale. Concurrency: a single in-flight refresh Promise is shared across concurrent callers, so a thundering-herd at startup or after expiry no longer fans out into N parallel token exchanges; a failed refresh unpins itself so the next caller retries cleanly. Documentation: tokenCacheBase.ts module doc now states 'these are *application* credentials issued by an OAuth2 client-credentials grant — there is no per-user state, the same token is valid for every server-handled request, and request-scoped storage would only burn provider rate limits. SvelteKit/CF Workers may run multiple isolates; each isolate keeping its own cache is acceptable since token endpoints are designed for that fan-out.' Both WCL and Blizzard providers go through the same factory (resolved blizzard_token_cache_singleton in prior commit). Commit d9cd5d2.

reflect.report:
## Reflect
Earlier clusters (component_responsibility, type_discipline, dependency_prune, route_handler_boilerplate, convention_drift) used small commits with svelte-check + vitest gates. Reuse for new themes.

What worked: small focused commits, type narrowing, subdir reorg, single-purpose helpers (apiResponses, wclGraphQL).
What did not work: blanket refactors, coverage-only tests.
Lessons: re-review after batches, careful skip attestations, auto-clusters surface real groups.
Anti-patterns: coverage-only tests, behavior-changing refactors, barrels that blur module boundaries.

strategist_briefing.executive_summary: Strict 74.8/100 after import. test_strategy 32, high_level_elegance 54, design_coherence 72, error_consistency 73, mid_level_elegance 73 below target. 348 review items.
strategist_briefing.observe_guidance: Inspect review findings grouped by file. Identify root-cause clusters: missing tests around pure lib modules; orchestration heaviness in route handlers.
strategist_briefing.reflect_guidance: Reuse pattern from earlier clusters (component_responsibility, type_discipline): small focused commits per concern, validated by svelte-check + vitest.
strategist_briefing.organize_guidance: Order: tests first; then orchestration simplification; then error/design/mid-level fixes.
strategist_briefing.sense_check_guidance: After each batch run npm run check and vitest. Re-review the touched dimension after ~5 fixes.

commit_log.note: Extracted WCL GraphQL, OAuth client_credentials, DB error wrapper, and JSON response helpers; standardized 9 endpoints on apiError/apiOk; tightened entityData types via discriminated union.
Added clientLog helper, standardized character lookup parameter order, documented API error contracts.
Bulk error-handling unification + recents store refactor + score-source registry; resolves 11 review findings.

Reorganize src/lib by domain (auth/data/ui/stores/calculations); shared db/connection.ts factory; document dual-DB schemas at three layers; align schema.ts with production (healerCompositions denormalised columns + deathHotspots); add missing userCharacters table; drop dead components and orphaned drizzle/schema.ts.
Drop dead auth duplicates from raid schema.
route_handler_boilerplate cluster closed: factory + apiOk unification.
type_discipline cluster: any -> concrete types at seams. component_responsibility cluster: components grouped into feature subdirs and console.error -> logClientError.
